symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden throat-closing (globus) sensation with difficulty swallowing
symptoms.onset: sudden, this evening after a holiday toast

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Globus is a classic anxiety/panic-associated sensation, especially with an acute emotional trigger.
A throat-closing sensation mandates active exclusion of a true airway process; it cannot be assumed benign remotely.
Chest tightness is common in panic/autonomic arousal.
Low pre-test probability given the airway-dominant, emotionally-triggered picture, but 'crushing' pressure is not dismissible from history alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: No known allergy and no new allergen/food/medication/sting exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a known allergy or a new exposure lowers anaphylaxis probability but does NOT exclude idiopathic/other angioedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Able to speak in full (breathless) sentences and sip water

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Phonation and swallowing indicate the airway is not critically occluded at this moment — reassuring but not exclusionary; laryngeal oedema can still be evolving.

symptoms.symptom_name: Racing/pounding heart, shakiness and a sense of impending dread

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic arousal with a sense of dread is a core feature of a panic response.

symptoms.symptom_name: Holiday/estrangement trigger with recurrent milder holiday episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxious mood / acute somatic panic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A specific emotional cue (toast to 'family') on a background of ambiguous grief, with recurrent holiday episodes, strongly supports an adjustment/somatic-panic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Grief, guilt and low mood; self-harm screen negative on sensitive questioning

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxious mood / acute somatic panic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prominent grief/guilt tied to the estrangement supports the adjustment-disorder driver; a self-harm screen is mandatory even though it is negative here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oropharyngeal examination — clear oropharynx, no uvular/lip/pharyngeal swelling, no stridor, clear breath sounds, no accessory-muscle use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: SEALED clinician finding: a clear oropharynx with no stridor and clear breath sounds is what excludes the organic airway mimic in person — precisely the examination a telehealth AI cannot perform.
A normal airway examination with a patent airway confirms the throat-closing sensation is functional/somatic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vitals — BP 145/88, HR 105, RR 22, SpO2 99%, afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: SEALED clinician measurement: mild tachycardia/tachypnoea with a mildly raised BP fits sympathetic/panic arousal.
A normal SpO2 (99%) argues against significant hypoxic airway compromise — but this is a clinician measurement unavailable to the AI over chat.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Holiday-triggered somatic panic with ambiguous grief
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxious mood / acute somatic panic (globus)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of an emotional cue, autonomic dread, recurrent holiday episodes and prominent grief points strongly to a psychosocial mechanism — once the organic airway mimic has been excluded in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person oropharyngeal examination and airway assessment (same day)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only reliable way to exclude angioedema/laryngeal oedema; compensates for the examination the telehealth AI cannot perform.
Objective vitals cannot be measured over chat and are needed to confirm the patient is not hypoxic or haemodynamically compromised.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ECG (if chest pressure features are concerning on in-person review)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Low pre-test probability of cardiac cause, but the 'crushing' chest description warrants an ECG at the in-person visit if any concerning features remain.
No allergen exposure or urticaria; a real-time telehealth tryptase is impossible and routine allergy testing is not indicated for this presentation.
